concepts.name: Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A variable whose value is a numerical outcome of a random phenomenon.

concepts.name: Discrete Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A random variable that can only take a finite or countably infinite number of values.

concepts.name: Continuous Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A random variable that can take any value within a given range.

concepts.name: Probability Mass Function
concepts.short_definition: A function that gives the probability that a discrete random variable is exactly equal to some value.

concepts.name: Probability Density Function
concepts.short_definition: A function whose value at any given point can be interpreted as representing the relative likelihood for this random variable to take on a given value.

concepts.name: Cumulative Distribution Function
concepts.short_definition: A function that gives the probability that a random variable is less than or equal to a certain value.

concepts.name: Expected Value
concepts.short_definition: The weighted average of all possible values of a random variable, weighted by their probabilities.

concepts.name: Variance
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the spread or dispersion of a random variable's distribution.

concepts.name: Bernoulli Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A discrete probability distribution of a single Bernoulli trial (an experiment with two possible outcomes).

concepts.name: Binomial Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A discrete probability distribution that represents the number of successes in a fixed number of independent Bernoulli trials.
